title: NER Pipelines in Enterprise
concept: Chaining Transformer NER with database lookups enables automated contract analysis and ticket routing. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Tokenization & Vocabulary.'

exercises.prompt: Before we link models to databases, what does the NLP term 'NER' stand for?
exercises.explain: NER stands for Named Entity Recognition. It is the task of finding and labeling key details like names, dates, and codes in text.

cards.title: AI at Scale
cards.body: Every day, large companies receive thousands of support emails and contracts. Reading them all by hand is slow and exhausting. What if we could build an assembly line that automatically reads the text, identifies the main topics, and instantly updates the company database? That is the power of enterprise NER pipelines!

cards.title: What is an Enterprise NER Pipeline?
cards.body: An Enterprise NER Pipeline is a system that chains together AI models and database lookups. First, a Transformer model identifies important names, dates, or product IDs in raw text. Then, a database query instantly matches those words to real customer accounts or contract files to take automated action.

cards.title: The Smart Mail Sorter
cards.body: Think of a Transformer model as a smart mail sorter who reads a letter and writes 'Urgent Invoice' on a sticky note. The database lookup is like a directory book. When the sorter hands the sticky note to the directory, it immediately finds the exact desk number where that invoice needs to go. Together, they get the mail to the right place without anyone manually reading the whole document.

cards.title: Routing Tickets in Action
cards.body:
Imagine a customer emails support saying: 'My account ACME-99 is locked.' 

1. The Transformer model reads the email and flags 'ACME-99' as an Account ID.
2. The pipeline automatically queries the database: SELECT account_owner FROM accounts WHERE id = 'ACME-99'.
3. The ticket is immediately routed to that owner's inbox without human triage.

cards.title: AI Doesn't Work Alone
cards.body: People often think that an AI model can do the entire job of updating records alone. In reality, the AI only highlights the important words (the entities). A separate program must take those words and look them up in a database to actually complete the task.

cards.title: Basic AI vs. Enterprise Pipeline
cards.body: Let's compare a basic AI model setup with a complete enterprise pipeline setup.

left.text: Only labels words in a text document (e.g., 'Apple' = Company). Does not take action.

right.text: Labels the word, queries the database, and automatically updates the client's file.

exercises.prompt: Why do we chain Transformer models with database lookups in enterprise applications?

options.text: To connect the extracted text entities to real-world business records and actions
Because database lookups make the AI model train faster
To replace the AI model entirely with standard SQL code
To turn text documents into video files

exercises.explain: Chaining combines the linguistic intelligence of Transformers with the factual, updated data stored in your company's databases.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An enterprise AI pipeline removes the need for traditional databases entirely.
exercises.explain: AI finds the text labels, but traditional databases are still required to hold structured business records like prices and emails.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing part of the enterprise AI workflow:
exercises.sentence: After the Transformer model finds a client's name in a contract, the pipeline queries a DB_PLACEHOLDER to retrieve their account history.
exercises.explain: Extracting the name is only the first step. To find the actual customer account, the pipeline must query a database using that name.

exercises.prompt: Which dialogue shows an automated enterprise pipeline in action?

options.text:
User: 'Where is my order?'
AI: 'Please tell me your order number so I can print it out and hand it to a manager.'

User: 'Where is order #404?'
AI: '(Extracts "Order #404" -> Queries DB) It looks like order #404 was shipped yesterday!'

User: 'Where is my order?'
AI: 'I do not know, and I cannot check any database systems.'

exercises.explain: The AI assistant represents a modern, automated enterprise pipeline that identifies the entity (Order #404) and automatically looks up the tracking status.

exercises.prompt: How does an enterprise NER pipeline automatically route incoming support tickets or process business contracts?

options.text: By extracting entities with NER and looking up their business records in a database
By manually printing the tickets and filing them in cabinets
By deleting the documents to save storage space
By rewriting the contracts using random words

exercises.explain: Awesome job! Combining Named Entity Recognition (NER) with enterprise databases turns raw text into actionable business steps instantly. Next up: Tokenization & Vocabulary.
